pattern: Swapping any two indices an even distance apart, repeated as often as you like, lets you freely reorder the even-indexed characters among themselves and, separately, the odd-indexed characters among themselves -- so equality only depends on whether the two strings' even-position and odd-position character multisets match.
hints: A swap always moves characters between two positions of the same parity (both even or both odd) -- positions never cross from even to odd or back.

Given unlimited swaps within a parity class, any arrangement of that class's characters is reachable -- so what matters isn't position, only which characters occupy the even slots and which occupy the odd slots.
Compare the sorted characters at even indices of both strings, and separately the sorted characters at odd indices -- both must match for the strings to be transformable into each other.

edge_inputs.description: same multiset overall but split wrong between even and odd slots
even-indexed characters match but odd-indexed ones don't
longer string needing a full within-parity rearrangement

stress.description: random strings over a small alphabet, some solvable and some not
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    s1 = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    base = list(s1)
    if rng.random() < 0.5:
        evens = [base[i] for i in range(0, n, 2)]
        odds = [base[i] for i in range(1, n, 2)]
        rng.shuffle(evens)
        rng.shuffle(odds)
        s2 = list(base)
        s2[0::2] = evens
        s2[1::2] = odds
        s2 = ''.join(s2)
    else:
        s2 = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    return [s1, s2]
